content: Treatment requires intensive insulin therapy via multiple daily injections (MDI) or continuous subcutaneous insulin infusion (CSII/pump). Basal-bolus regimen: long-acting basal insulin (glargine, detemir, degludec) once/twice daily plus rapid-acting bolus insulin (lispro, aspart, glulisine) before meals. Insulin dosing based on carbohydrate counting (typically 1 unit per 10-15g carbs) and correction factor (1 unit lowers glucose by 30-50 mg/dL). Continuous glucose monitoring (CGM) strongly recommended; target time-in-range (70-180 mg/dL) >70%, time-below-range <4%. Hybrid closed-loop systems (automated insulin delivery) improve glycemic control and reduce hypoglycemia. Adjunct therapies: pramlintide for postprandial control; SGLT2 inhibitors off-label with caution for DKA risk. Target HbA1c <7% individualized.
